question_title: Will Dmitry Medvedev be placed first on United Russia's federal candidate list approved at the June 28, 2026 congress?
summary: This question presents genuine uncertainty about a decision that Putin will make at the June 28 congress (10 days away). The evidence is meaningfully split. In favor of Medvedev being first: the January 2026 consensus from multiple credible Russian outlets (RBC, Kommersant, Vedomosti) placed Medvedev first; his troika proposal is described as "working and supported within the party" (Meduza, June 10); and he's party chairman with a 2011/2016 precedent. Against: the most recent Vedomosti report (June 11) cites three sources saying his inclusion is "under question" and floats a list without him; Putin specifically blocked Medvedev in 2021; internal opposition from Kiriyenko exists; and his trust ratings are mediocre. The decomposition P(on list) × P(first | on list) suggests ~50-55% × ~85-90% ≈ 44-48%. I estimate 46%, slightly below 50% due to the recency weight of the June 11 doubts and the 2021 exclusion precedent.

factors.description: January 2026 base scenario from RBC, Kommersant, and Vedomosti consistently placed Medvedev first in the federal list
Medvedev is UR party chairman - institutional default for heading the list; he did so in 2011 and 2016
Meduza (June 10): Medvedev's troika variant (Medvedev, Sobyanin, Poddubny) described as 'working and supported within the party'
UNN/ISW reported 'Putin has identified' Medvedev as #1 in top five (though may be based on January scenario)
Vedomosti June 11: Three sources say Medvedev's inclusion is 'under question'; floated a list WITHOUT Medvedev (Lavrov, Sobyanin, Bolilaya, Golovin, Poddubny)
2021 precedent: Putin specifically blocked Medvedev from heading the list, replacing him with collective of 5 figures
Internal opposition from Kiriyenko (fears strengthening Medvedev as Putin successor) and potentially Volodin
Medvedev's trust rating dropped to 34% in April 2026; his harsh rhetoric 'scares people away' per sources
Presidential administration source says troika is 'just one of the variants' with 'no clarity on the architecture' - decision not yet final

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**Will Dmitry Medvedev be placed first on United Russia's federal candidate list at the June 28, 2026 congress?**

### Probability: **0.46** (46%)

### Key Reasoning

This is a genuinely uncertain question about a decision that only Putin can make, with the congress 10 days away and no official announcement.

**Evidence FOR Medvedev first (~46%):**
- Multiple credible Russian outlets (RBC, Kommersant, Vedomosti) converged on Medvedev as #1 in the "base scenario" from January 2026
- His troika proposal (Medvedev, Sobyanin, Poddubny) is described as "working and supported within the party" (Meduza, June 10)
- He's United Russia's party chairman — the institutional default for heading the list
- He headed the list in 2011 and 2016

**Evidence AGAINST (~54%):**
- Most recent Vedomosti report (June 11): Three sources say his inclusion is "under question," floating a list without him entirely
- **2021 precedent**: Putin specifically blocked Medvedev from the list, replacing him with a collective of 5 figures (Shoigu, Lavrov, etc.)
- Internal opposition from Kiriyenko (fears strengthening Medvedev as potential successor)
- Trust rating dropped to 34% in April; his harsh rhetoric "scares people away"
- Presidential administration source says "no clarity on the architecture"

**Decomposition**: P(on list at all) ≈ 50-55% × P(first | on list) ≈ 85-90% = **~44-48%**
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Dmitry Medvedev be placed first on United Russia's federal candidate list approved at the June 28, 2026 congress?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/44107)

[Medvedev is number one: Putin has identified the top five leaders of "United Russia" for the State Duma elections - media](https://unn.ua/en/news/medvedev-is-number-one-putin-has-identified-the-top-five-leaders-of-united-russia-for-the-state-duma-elections-media)

[Meduza’s sources say Dmitry Medvedev is tired of being a ‘troll’ and wants back in Russia’s political spotlight — Meduza](https://meduza.io/en/feature/2026/01/21/meduza-s-sources-say-dmitry-medvedev-is-tired-of-being-a-troll-and-wants-back-in-russia-s-political-spotlight)
[Кто может повести список «Единой России» на выборы в Госдуму](https://www.vedomosti.ru/politics/articles/2026/06/11/1205098-kto-mozhet-povesti)

[«Полный долбо*****»: выборы в Госдуму через три месяца, а у «Единой России» нет ни программы, ни ключевых тезисов кампании. Список партии хочет возглавить Медведев — в компании с Собяниным и «военкором» Поддубным — Meduza](https://meduza.io/feature/2026/06/10/polnyy-dolbo-vybory-v-gosdumu-cherez-tri-mesyatsa-a-u-edinoy-rossii-net-ni-programmy-ni-klyuchevyh-tezisov-kampanii)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if Dmitry Medvedev is named in the first position of the general-federal part (общефедеральная часть) of United Russia's State Duma candidate list as approved at the party's congress whose first stage is held on June 28, 2026, according to official party communications (er.ru), [TASS](https://tass.ru/politika), or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). It resolves NO if the approved federal list is led by someone other than Medvedev, or if Medvedev is not in the general-federal part of the approved list at all. If the congress's first stage is postponed such that no federal list is officially approved before June 28, 2026, the question resolves NO.
fine_print: "First position" refers to the candidate listed at number 1 in the general-federal (nationwide) portion of the list, as distinct from regional groups. If the general-federal part contains a single name and that name is Medvedev, the question resolves YES. If credible sources conflict on the ordering, the official United Russia announcement (er.ru) takes precedence. A leaked, draft, or pre-congress preliminary list does not count; only the list formally approved at the congress counts.

input.assessment:
After reflection, I'm adjusting downward from 52% to 46%. The factor analysis correctly identifies that the negative evidence (more recent June 11 Vedomosti report, 2021 precedent of Putin blocking Medvedev, internal opposition) slightly outweighs the positive evidence (earlier January consensus, institutional default as party chairman).

The key decomposition: P(on list at all) × P(first | on list). If Medvedev is on the list, he's almost certainly first (~85-90%). The real uncertainty is P(on list) ≈ 50-55%. This gives P(YES) ≈ 0.50 × 0.88 ≈ 0.44, or 0.55 × 0.88 ≈ 0.48.

The most recent reporting (June 10-11) paints a picture of genuine indecision at the Kremlin level with 10 days until the congress. The earlier consensus from January provides mild upward pressure, but Putin's track record of overriding Medvedev's ambitions (2021) and the specific recent doubt from Vedomosti pull the estimate below 50%.
input.tool_audit: All tools performed well. research() and fetch_url were the most valuable, providing detailed Russian-language source analysis. No failures.
input.process_reflection: The reflection metrics were helpful in identifying overconfidence in the pro-Medvedev direction. The gap analysis correctly flagged that my initial estimate was too high relative to the evidence.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Checks Performed

### Hallucinated evidence
All 9 factors were verified against the research trace:

- **Factor 1** (January RBC/Kommersant/Vedomosti base scenario): Confirmed by the research tool results, which state "По данным «Ведомостей», РБК и BFM.ru, Дмитрий Медведев возглавит федеральный список" and the web search results referencing the RBC January 2026 article.
- **Factor 2** (Party chairman, headed list 2011/2016): Confirmed by Wikipedia, worldview research on 2021 election history, and multiple trace sources.
- **Factor 3** (Meduza June 10 troika): Confirmed by the Meduza fetch_url result describing the Medvedev-Sobyanin-Poddubny troika as "working and supported within the party."
- **Factor 4** (UNN/ISW): Confirmed by UNN fetch_url citing ISW, with appropriate caveat about possible January origin.
- **Factor 5** (Vedomosti June 11 "under question"): Confirmed by Vedomosti fetch_url showing three sources questioning inclusion, and the alternative list of Lavrov/Sobyanin/Bolilaya/Golovin/Poddubny.
- **Factor 6** (2021 exclusion): Confirmed by worldview research and Riddle Russia article quoting "Putin prevented him from doing so."
- **Factor 7** (Kiriyenko/Volodin opposition): Confirmed by Meduza January 2026 article.
- **Factor 8** (34% trust rating): Confirmed by Vedomosti June 11 fetch result.
- **Factor 9** (Admin source "just one variant"): Confirmed by Meduza June 10 fetch result.

No hallucinations found.

### Double-counting
Factors 1 and 4 both draw on January-era reporting about Medvedev heading the list. The UNN/ISW article (Factor 4) appears to relay the same RBC base scenario. However, the agent gave Factor 4 low confidence (0.4) and a modest logit (+0.5), partially mitigating this. Mild concern but not consequential enough to change the forecast.

### Wrong-direction factors
All factor signs are consistent with their descriptions. No issues.

### Resolution criteria alignment
The agent correctly engages with the specific resolution criteria (first position in the general-federal part, approved at the congress, per official party communications). The decomposition P(on list) × P(first | on list) directly addresses the resolution requirement.

### Contradictory assessment
The assessment reaches 46%, which aligns with the net negative factor sum (-0.31 net logits, implying ~42.3%). The agent explains the small upward adjustment from factor-implied probability as giving weight to institutional defaults. No contradiction.

### Counterargument quality
The counterargument is strong — it constructs a coherent positive case citing the January consensus, institutional logic, Meduza's characterization, and the possibility of deliberate misdirection. It also provides specific, quantified conditions for changing the estimate. Not a token gesture.

### Self-assessment confidence
0.35 confidence is appropriate given genuinely conflicting signals from credible sources, with the decision unmade 10 days before the congress.

### Probability assessment
The 46% estimate is reasonable. My independent assessment: P(on list at all) ≈ 50-55% based on the conflicting January consensus vs. June doubt; P(first | on list) ≈ 85-90% given the institutional logic. This yields 44-48%, which brackets the agent's 46%. No meaningful disagreement.

### Tool audit
Minor discrepancy: the agent claims "No failures" but wikipedia had 1 error out of 9 calls (11.1% error rate). This doesn't affect the forecast quality.

### Worldview consistency
No worldview forecasts exist for this question. Worldview research entries are neutral on Medvedev's chances, consistent with the agent's near-50% estimate.
